examples: A species of alumroot (Heuchera sanguinea) was native to the southwest United States and Mexico.
A species of alumroot (Heuchera sanguinea) native to the southwest United States and Mexico, widely cultivated in gardens for its clusters of small, bell-shaped, red to white flowers.
The frilly leaves of coral bells (Heuchera) come in shades of purple, chartreuse or even orange.
